prestazioni: Engine swap to a C20XE 2.0L 16V Red Top (204hp) from the Opel Calibra/Vectra, retaining the original Manta B-style engine mounts for a direct fit. Pair with a custom 4-2-1 stainless steel exhaust manifold by Janspeed Engineering, featuring 45mm primaries and 60mm collectors, mated to a Milltek Sport 2.5-inch cat-back system with a 304-grade stainless steel resonator delete for aggressive tone.

Forced induction via a Garrett GT2860RS turbocharger (0.63 A/R turbine housing) on a custom cast-aluminum manifold by Turbo Dynamics, running 1.0 bar of boost with a custom ECU tune by DTAfast S80 Pro standalone unit, mapped for 98 RON fuel with a 220cc/min Bosch EV14 high-impedance injector upgrade and a Walbro 255 LPH in-tank fuel pump.

Suspension overhaul with Bilstein B14 PSS9 coilovers (adjustable damping and ride height) paired with Eibach Pro-Kit springs (-30mm front, -25mm rear), reinforced with polybushings from SuperPro (part numbers SPF1048K and SPF1049K for front/rear control arms) and a Whiteline 24mm adjustable rear sway bar (part number BMR75) to eliminate body roll.
estetica: Custom widebody conversion using fiberglass fender flares from a Vauxhall Frontera Sport A-pillar kit by KBD Body Kits, extended 50mm per side to accommodate 8.5J ET20 Enkei PF05 wheels (16x8) wrapped in 225/50R16 Toyo R888R semi-slick tires for track use.

Front bumper lip spoiler from the Opel Manta 400 homologation special, modified with a carbon fiber splitter by Seibon Carbon, paired with a custom rear diffuser by Maxton Design (part number MA010) featuring integrated LED reverse lights and a central exhaust cutout for the Milltek system.

Interior retrim with Recaro SRD bucket seats (Opel-specific mounting brackets) upholstered in Alcantara with red stitching by Cobra Seats, paired with a Momo Prototipo steering wheel (350mm diameter) and a custom roll cage by Safety Devices (part number SD0456) with bolt-in X-brace for chassis rigidity.
